content.problemStatement:
You know that 111 kg of pulp can be used to make 100010001000 pages and 111 notebook consists of 100100100 pages.

Suppose a notebook factory receives NNN kg of pulp, how many notebooks can be made from that?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single integer NNN - the weight of the pulp the factory has (in kgs).


Output Format
For each test case, output the number of notebooks that can be made using NNN kgs of pulp.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the weight of pulp in kilograms.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the maximum number of notebooks that can be produced from N kilograms of pulp.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Maximum allowed N: 1000 kg produces 1000*10 = 10000 notebooks.

solution.approach: From the statement we infer that 1 kg of pulp can be used to make 10 notebooks. Therefore for any given N, the answer is simply N multiplied by 10. The algorithm reads each N, computes N*10, and prints the result. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time multiplication per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
